en: you pardon me my lord i never got your name i was wondering if we'd meet again
so you might discern if my wit is acceptable my man is genteel but you were eavesdropping
ko: 다시 만날 수 있을까 했어요. 제 재치가 괜찮은지 판단해보시려고요. 이 남자 점잖긴 한데 엿듣고 있었군요.

en: pardon me is a formal way to say i'm sorry she's apologizing for listening to his private
ko: pardon me는 i'm sorry의 격식체야. 사적인 대화를 듣고 있었던 것에 대한 사과야.

en: conversation when anthony realizes kate was listening instead of directly asking her what's
ko: Anthony가 Kate가 듣고 있었다는 걸 깨닫고 what's your name 대신

en: your name he uses the alternative i never got your name this is a way to say they haven't been
ko: I never got your name이라는 대안을 써. 아직 공식적으로 소개되지 않았다는 뜻이야. 문장에서

en: formally introduced yet did you notice that in the sentence i was wondering if we'd meet again
the word would is contracted and you can barely hear it it's very common for native speakers to
ko: I was wondering if we'd meet again에서 would가 축약돼서 거의 안 들려. 원어민들이

en: contract auxiliary words such as would did and hat discern in this context means to decide
ko: would, did, had 같은 조동사를 축약하는 건 아주 흔해. discern은

en: if someone is witty they are clever very good with words and able to think quickly genteel
ko: 이 맥락에서 판단하다를 의미해. witty는 재치 있고 말을 잘하며

en: is an old-fashioned word for elegant to eavesdrop means to secretly listen to a conversation
ko: 빠르게 생각하는 거야. genteel은 우아하다의 고어체야. eavesdrop은 몰래 대화를
